draft.intro: Meta shipped its most significant Quest update in two years, Google DeepMind demonstrated real-time 3D scene reconstruction from monocular video, and enterprise XR deployments crossed a new milestone with Boeing's factory-floor rollout.
draft.notes: Strong week for spatial hardware announcements. The DeepMind paper is particularly relevant — real-time monocular 3D reconstruction has direct implications for passthrough-based MR headsets.

items.title: Meta Unveils Quest 4 with Pancake Optics and Full-Color Passthrough at 4K Per Eye
items.description: Meta today announced Quest 4, featuring pancake optics, 4K per-eye resolution, and full-color video passthrough powered by a new dedicated ISP chip.
items.summary: Meta announced Quest 4 with pancake optics delivering 4K per-eye resolution and full-color video passthrough via a dedicated image signal processor, targeting a $499 price point for holiday 2026.

items.title: Samsung Unveils Lightweight AR Glasses Prototype at Display Week
items.description: Samsung showed a 42-gram AR glasses prototype with micro-OLED displays and a 52-degree field of view.
items.summary: Samsung demonstrated a 42-gram AR glasses prototype at Display Week featuring micro-OLED waveguide displays with a 52-degree field of view, its lightest form factor to date.

items.title: Varjo Releases XR-4 Focal Edition with Foveated Varifocal Display
items.description: Varjo launched XR-4 Focal Edition with varifocal displays driven by eye tracking, reducing vergence-accommodation conflict.
items.summary: Varjo launched the XR-4 Focal Edition, combining foveated rendering with varifocal displays driven by eye tracking to reduce vergence-accommodation conflict in professional simulation workflows.

items.title: Meta Horizon OS Opens to Third-Party Headset Makers, ASUS and Lenovo First Partners
items.description: Meta opened Horizon OS licensing to ASUS and Lenovo, who will ship MR headsets running Meta's platform in 2027.
items.summary: Meta opened Horizon OS licensing to third-party hardware makers, with ASUS and Lenovo confirmed as launch partners planning MR headsets for 2027 — a direct play to establish Android-like platform dominance in spatial computing.

items.title: Niantic Launches Large Geospatial Model for AR Cloud Anchoring
items.description: Niantic released its Visual Positioning System powered by a large geospatial model trained on its Lightship scanning data.
items.summary: Niantic released a large geospatial model powering centimeter-accurate AR cloud anchoring, trained on scanning data from 10 million Lightship locations worldwide.

items.title: Qualcomm Snapdragon XR3 Platform Promises 12 TOPS for On-Device Spatial AI
items.description: Qualcomm announced Snapdragon XR3, delivering 12 TOPS of AI compute for spatial understanding tasks on-device.
items.summary: Qualcomm announced the Snapdragon XR3 platform delivering 12 TOPS of dedicated AI compute for on-device spatial understanding, hand tracking, and scene reconstruction in next-generation headsets.

items.title: Google DeepMind Achieves Real-Time 3D Scene Reconstruction from Monocular Video
items.description: DeepMind published research showing real-time dense 3D reconstruction from a single camera feed at 40fps on mobile hardware.
items.summary: Google DeepMind published research demonstrating dense 3D scene reconstruction from monocular video at 40fps on mobile hardware, eliminating the need for depth sensors in spatial understanding pipelines.

items.title: NVIDIA Research Introduces Instant NeRF 2.0 with Real-Time Editing
items.description: NVIDIA Research released Instant NeRF 2.0, enabling interactive editing of neural radiance fields in real-time.
items.summary: NVIDIA Research released Instant NeRF 2.0, enabling interactive object insertion, removal, and relighting within neural radiance fields at 60fps — a significant step toward real-time spatial content authoring.

items.title: MIT CSAIL Publishes Foundation Model for 6-DOF Object Pose Estimation
items.description: MIT CSAIL trained a foundation model for 6-DOF pose estimation that generalizes across 10,000 object categories without fine-tuning.
items.summary: MIT CSAIL published a foundation model for 6-DOF object pose estimation that generalizes across 10,000 categories zero-shot, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on BOP benchmark without per-object fine-tuning.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude 4.5 Opus with Extended Thinking and Agentic Capabilities
items.description: Anthropic launched Claude 4.5 Opus, its most capable model featuring extended thinking chains and improved tool use for agentic workflows.
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude 4.5 Opus with extended thinking and substantially improved agentic capabilities, setting new benchmarks on SWE-bench and GPQA.

items.title: Google Announces Gemini 2.5 Ultra with Native Multimodal Reasoning
items.description: Google released Gemini 2.5 Ultra with native multimodal reasoning across text, image, video, and audio in a single forward pass.
items.summary: Google released Gemini 2.5 Ultra featuring native multimodal reasoning across text, image, video, and audio simultaneously, outperforming prior models on cross-modal benchmarks by 15%.

items.title: Boeing Deploys 5,000 HoloLens Units Across Factory Floor for Assembly Guidance
items.description: Boeing completed its largest XR deployment, rolling out 5,000 HoloLens headsets for step-by-step assembly guidance across three manufacturing plants.
items.summary: Boeing completed its largest enterprise XR deployment with 5,000 HoloLens units providing step-by-step holographic assembly guidance across three manufacturing plants, reporting 30% reduction in assembly errors.

items.title: Siemens Integrates Spatial Computing into Industrial Digital Twin Platform
items.description: Siemens added AR visualization and spatial anchoring to its Xcelerator digital twin platform for on-site maintenance workflows.
items.summary: Siemens integrated AR visualization and spatial anchoring into its Xcelerator digital twin platform, enabling maintenance technicians to overlay real-time equipment data onto physical machinery via headset.

items.title: Unity Releases Polyspatial 2.0 with Cross-Platform Spatial UI Framework
items.description: Unity shipped PolySpatial 2.0 with a unified spatial UI framework targeting visionOS, Quest, and WebXR from a single codebase.
items.summary: Unity shipped PolySpatial 2.0 with a cross-platform spatial UI framework enabling developers to target visionOS, Quest, and WebXR from a single codebase with platform-native interactions.

items.title: Khronos Group Ratifies OpenXR 1.2 with Improved Hand Tracking and Composition Layers
items.description: The Khronos Group ratified OpenXR 1.2, adding standardized hand tracking, composition layers, and scene understanding extensions.
items.summary: Khronos Group ratified OpenXR 1.2, standardizing hand tracking, composition layers, and scene understanding APIs across runtimes — reducing fragmentation for cross-platform XR developers.

items.title: Stanford HAI Publishes Longitudinal Study on Spatial Computing's Effect on Collaboration
items.description: Stanford HAI released a 2-year longitudinal study finding that spatial computing collaboration tools improved team performance by 23% vs video conferencing.
items.summary: Stanford HAI published a 2-year longitudinal study across 1,200 participants finding that spatial computing collaboration tools improved team task performance by 23% and reduced meeting fatigue compared to video conferencing.
